title: Fix text color visibility and add Download as Image (PNG/JPG) feature

requirements.summary: Fix text color visibility in the resume preview and form so that all text is clearly legible against its background in both light and dark themes
acceptanceCriteria: All text in the resume preview is clearly visible and readable against its background
All form field labels, inputs, and placeholder text are legible against their backgrounds
No text appears invisible or blends into the background in either light or dark mode

file_operations.description: Audit and adjust CSS custom properties for foreground and background colors to ensure sufficient contrast ratios (WCAG AA minimum 4.5:1 for normal text) in both light and dark themes. Review all color token definitions including --foreground, --background, --card-foreground, --primary-foreground, --muted-foreground, and other text-related tokens.

Audit all inline styles and className assignments for text elements to ensure foreground colors contrast sufficiently with their backgrounds. Replace any hardcoded color values that may cause visibility issues with CSS custom properties or Tailwind utility classes that respect the theme. Pay special attention to header text, section titles, body text, and contact information.

Review and fix text color visibility for all form field labels, input placeholders, and helper text. Ensure all text elements use appropriate foreground color tokens that provide sufficient contrast against card and background colors in both themes.
Audit label and input text colors to ensure they are legible against form backgrounds. Replace any problematic color assignments with theme-aware tokens.
Audit label and input text colors to ensure they are legible against form backgrounds. Replace any problematic color assignments with theme-aware tokens.

requirements.summary: Add a 'Download as Image' button to the resume builder UI that captures the rendered resume preview as a PNG image and triggers a browser download
acceptanceCriteria: A 'Download as Image' button is visible in the header alongside existing action buttons
Clicking the button captures the full resume preview as a PNG image
The captured image includes the profile photo if one is uploaded
The download triggers automatically without opening a new tab

file_operations.description: Add html2canvas library as a dependency to enable DOM-to-canvas conversion for capturing the resume preview as an image. Use version ^1.4.1 or latest stable.

Create a custom hook that uses html2canvas to capture the ResumePreview component DOM element as a canvas, convert it to a PNG blob, and trigger a browser download as 'resume.png'. The hook should accept a ref to the preview container element and return a download function. Handle profile photos by ensuring cross-origin images are properly loaded. Include error handling for failed captures.

Add a 'Download as Image' button in the sticky header alongside the existing 'Save as HTML' button. Import and use the new useDownloadImage hook, passing a ref to the ResumePreview container. Wire the button's onClick handler to trigger the image download function. Keep the existing 'Save as HTML' button intact. Ensure the button uses consistent styling with existing action buttons.

Accept and forward a ref prop to the outermost container element of the resume preview to enable html2canvas to target and capture the full rendered preview. Ensure the ref is properly typed as a React.RefObject<HTMLDivElement>.
